heroHeadline: PLA Resumes Strait Pressure After Two-Sessions Lull; US Intelligence Revises 2027 Invasion Assessment
heroSubtitle: Following a two-week tactical pause during China's annual 'Two Sessions,' the PLA resumed frequent operations in the Taiwan Strait — deploying 8 vessels and 24 aircraft across the median line. U.S. intelligence agencies' 2026 annual threat report assesses China does not plan a 2027 invasion, instead pursuing control through coercion and gray-zone pressure. US officials confirm the Iran conflict has not delayed arms shipments to Taiwan, while Trump and Japanese PM Takaichi reaffirmed commitment to Taiwan Strait peace and stability.
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, Reuters, AP, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
